title: Chain-of-Agents: End-to-End Agent Foundation Models via Multi-Agent Distillation and Agentic RL
summary: This paper introduces Chain-of-Agents (CoA), a novel method enabling a single large language model to solve complex problems end-to-end by dynamically simulating collaboration among multiple specialized agents and tools. This approach addresses the inefficiencies of traditional multi-agent systems by using multi-agent distillation and agentic reinforcement learning to create Agent Foundation Models (AFMs), which achieve state-of-the-art performance. The research, including models, code, and data, is fully open-sourced.
key_findings: Introduces Chain-of-Agents (CoA), a paradigm for end-to-end multi-agent-like problem-solving within a single LLM.
Addresses limitations of existing multi-agent systems, such as computational inefficiency and lack of data-centric learning.
Utilizes a multi-agent distillation framework to generate data for agentic supervised fine-tuning.
Applies agentic reinforcement learning on verifiable tasks to further enhance CoA capabilities.
Resulting Agent Foundation Models (AFMs) achieve new state-of-the-art performance on web and code agent benchmarks.
The entire research, including model weights, code, and training data, is open-sourced.
methodology: The paper proposes Chain-of-Agents (CoA) and trains Agent Foundation Models (AFMs) using a two-stage process: multi-agent distillation to create trajectories for agentic supervised fine-tuning, followed by agentic reinforcement learning on verifiable tasks.
